architecture_summary: BLIP-2 bootstraps vision-language pre-training from frozen pre-trained image encoders and frozen large language models using a lightweight Querying Transformer (Q-Former) trained in two stages to bridge the modality gap.

training_details.compute_surface: using a single 16-A100(40G) machine, our largest model with ViT-g and FlanT5-XXL requires less than 6 days for the first stage and less than 3 days for the second stage

novel_contributions: a generic and efficient pre-training strategy that bootstraps vision-language pre-training from off-the-shelf frozen pre-trained image encoders and frozen large language models
a lightweight Querying Transformer (Q-Former) pre-trained in two stages to bridge the modality gap
state-of-the-art performance on various vision-language tasks despite having significantly fewer trainable parameters than existing methods
zero-shot image-to-text generation that can follow natural language instructions

key_claims.claim: BLIP-2 is a generic and compute-efficient vision-language pre-training method that uses frozen pre-trained image encoders and frozen large language models.
The model bridges the modality gap with a lightweight Querying Transformer pre-trained in two stages: representation learning and generative learning.
BLIP-2 achieves state-of-the-art performance on various vision-language tasks while using significantly fewer trainable parameters than existing methods.
BLIP-2 outperforms Flamingo80B by 8.7% on zero-shot VQAv2 with 54x fewer trainable parameters.
A stronger image encoder or a stronger LLM both lead to better performance, supporting BLIP-2 as a generic method.
The first-stage representation learning is important; without it, the Q-Former fails to bridge the modality gap and zero-shot VQA performance drops substantially.
BLIP-2 achieves state-of-the-art performance on image captioning and image-text retrieval, and strong zero-shot VQA results.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.2. Image Captioning; 4.4. Image-Text Retrieval; 4.3. Visual Question Answering
